conditions: Major depressive disorder (recurrent, mild to moderate)
health.mental_health: Since the promotion, she has more frequent anxiety spikes before leadership meetings and intermittent low mood; she is worried she is “not management material,” has sleep fragmentation, and occasionally uses propranolol before presentations. She has had passive thoughts of quitting but denies intent to self-harm.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for fatigue and thyroid labs (TSH adjustment discussed)
Telehealth psychiatry follow-up for anxiety/depression medication check
Gynecology visit for perimenopausal symptoms and sleep concerns
Blood pressure recheck at a CVS MinuteClinic after elevated readings at work

finance.debt: Carries a Boston-area mortgage and a remaining balance on a home equity line used for a kitchen repair last year; also has a moderate credit card balance that grew during the stressful transition and a short-term personal loan for an emergency car repair.
investments: High-yield savings account for emergency fund
recent_transactions: Pharmacy refill for levothyroxine and sertraline
Paid for an online management coaching course subscription
Increased monthly transit pass and occasional rideshare charges after late nights at the office

relationships: Spouse: Vijay Reddy (supportive but frustrated by her long hours and irritability)
Adult daughter: Mira Reddy (college senior; calls home for advice but feels Ananya is distracted)
Close friend: Priya Nair (former coworker; confidante about work stress and mental health)
Older sister: Srilatha Reddy (lives in New Jersey; sometimes critical about Ananya “overworking”)
Neighbor: Linda O’Connor (occasionally checks in; invites her to community events she often declines)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive on Facebook and Instagram; posts occasional family photos and Boston-area outings but avoids mentioning work. Uses LinkedIn more actively since the promotion, liking leadership posts and following insurance industry groups.
private_conversations: Confided to Priya that she worries she was promoted to fill a gap and is “in over her head”
Discussed with Vijay the possibility of taking FMLA or stepping back from the manager role due to anxiety
Talked with Mira about not sleeping and feeling emotionally numb, and asked her not to tell extended family
Vented in a group text about a difficult employee performance issue and fear of an HR complaint

schedule.work_hours: Typically 8:30am–6:30pm weekdays; often logs back in 8:30pm–10:00pm to clear escalations and emails. On-call for severe claim escalations about 1 weekend per month.
regular_appointments: Therapy session every other Tuesday at 7:00pm (telehealth)
Quarterly thyroid labs and PCP follow-up
Monthly one-on-one with her manager (first Thursday morning)
Weekly team check-in meeting she leads (Mondays at 9:00am)
upcoming_events: Leadership training workshop in downtown Boston (two full days next month)
Performance review cycle meetings with two direct reports (scheduled over the next two weeks)
Family trip to visit her sister in New Jersey (tentative, dependent on workload)
Mira’s college graduation weekend planning call
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 6:15am, quick coffee and checks email before commuting; often skips a full lunch, snacks at her desk, and stays late to handle escalations. Evenings are split between catching up on work, short walks when she can, and trying to wind down with TV or reading, though sleep is inconsistent.

professional.company: Regional property & casualty insurer headquartered in Massachusetts (mid-size carrier with a Boston office)
projects: Implementing a new claims triage workflow to reduce cycle time
Coaching two newly hired adjusters through complex property loss files
Auditing reopened claims for compliance and documentation quality
Coordinating with SIU and legal on a small set of suspected fraud claims
professional.performance_issues: Struggling with delegation and conflict management; she double-checks her team’s work late at night, has missed a few internal deadlines, and received feedback that her meeting facilitation is overly detailed and tense. She is worried a recent complaint from a direct report could escalate to HR.
workplace_relationships: Manager: Tom Gallagher (supportive but pushing her to ‘think bigger’ and stop micromanaging)
Direct report: Jenna Morales (high performer; resents constant revisions and has hinted at transferring)
Direct report: Kevin O’Brien (needs structure; improved with coaching but still makes documentation errors)
Peer manager: Alicia Chen (friendly competitor; Ananya suspects she is being compared unfavorably)

hobbies: Long walks along the Charles River Esplanade
Cooking South Indian meals on weekends when she has energy
Reading mystery novels and leadership/communication books
Attending occasional classical music or cultural events in Cambridge
Gardening in small containers on her porch/balcony
habits: Two to three cups of coffee most weekdays, sometimes late afternoon to push through meetings
Stress snacking at work (chips/sweets) and skipping balanced lunches
Uses a sleep app and occasionally takes melatonin on nights before big presentations
Journals briefly after difficult employee conversations to manage rumination
Drinks a glass of wine a few nights a week to unwind, more during peak work stress
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with her spouse in a mortgaged condo in the Boston area; her adult daughter is usually away at school but visits on breaks. The home feels like a ‘second office’ lately because she often works from her laptop at the kitchen table.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked, largely vegetarian with occasional fish/chicken; tries to limit sugar for energy swings but struggles when stressed. Aims for lower sodium after borderline blood pressure readings.
